sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Valencia have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, managing 1 win, 3 draws, and 1 loss, with a recent 2-0 victory over Sporting Gijón indicating some attacking potential. Mallorca, on the other hand, have had an inconsistent run with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, but their 3-1 win against Elche highlights their ability to score. Both teams are struggling for consistency, with Valencia slightly better defensively in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, each team has secured a 1-goal victory, with Valencia winning 1-0 at home in March 2025 and Mallorca taking a 2-1 win in November 2024. Matches between these sides tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 meetings, suggesting a tight contest is likely today.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Valencia, sitting 17th with 15 points, have struggled offensively, scoring just 15 goals this season (13th in La Liga), while conceding 25 (17th). Mallorca, positioned 14th with 17 points, have a slightly better attack with 18 goals and a comparable defense, conceding 23. Mallorca's key player, Vedat Muriqi, with 9 goals, could be a decisive factor.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Valencia, Hugo Duro (5 goals) will be crucial in breaking Mallorca’s defense, while Pepelu’s midfield control (6.96 average rating) could dictate play. Mallorca’s Vedat Muriqi, in fine form with 4 goals in his last 5 matches, poses a significant threat, supported by Jan Virgili’s creative play (4 assists).

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (32% home win, 29% draw, 39% away win) and considering Mallorca’s slight edge in form and attacking output, they are marginally favored. Valencia’s home advantage at Mestalla and recent defensive solidity could make it close, but Mallorca’s clinical finishing might tilt the balance. Expect a low-scoring game with potential for a narrow away win.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Mallorca leads Valencia 1-0 at the Mestalla. The away side capitalized on their opportunity with a well-taken goal by Samú Costa in the 23rd minute. Valencia, despite dominating possession and creating more entries into the final third, failed to convert their chances into goals. Mallorca, on the other hand, made the most of their limited attacking forays to take the lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Valencia's attacking intent
halftimeSections.text: Valencia showcased significant control over the game, particularly in terms of territory and ball possession. They frequently pushed into the final third and penalty area but struggled with the final touch. Players like Luis Rioja and Pepelu showed creativity with key passes, yet the team couldn't find the back of the net. Their persistence in attack is evident, but they need to sharpen their finishing in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Mallorca's defensive resilience
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca adopted a more conservative approach, focusing on defending deep and striking on the counter. Their defensive structure held firm against Valencia's sustained pressure, with players like Vedat Muriqi and Martin Valjent contributing to clearances. The goal from Samú Costa highlighted their ability to be clinical despite limited possession, and their defense will likely remain a key factor in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Valencia dominate the early stages with consistent attacking spells, pushing forward with intent. Mallorca, however, gained momentum around the time of their goal, enjoying a brief period of control in attack. After conceding, Valencia regrouped and mounted several waves of pressure towards the latter part of the half, while Mallorca's attacking presence dwindled as they focused on protecting their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player performances
halftimeSections.text: For Valencia, Luis Rioja and Pepelu stood out with their ability to create opportunities, while Lucas Beltrán showed promise in dangerous areas. On Mallorca’s side, Samú Costa was the standout performer, not only scoring the decisive goal but also contributing defensively. Antonio Raíllo also played a crucial role with an assist and solid defensive work, which helped maintain their narrow advantage.
